title: The Doh Cial Network

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, iconic but not original at this point

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Repetition of catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Repetitive but still characteristically Homer

jokes.text: So what are we doing here? What, is it the thing where the family runs in and sits down? That's it? We flew you in and put you up for that?
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about the couch gag
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about celebrity cameos and the show's structure - genuinely funny deconstruction

jokes.text: Lethal inject her on the electric chair. Bart, it's okay to say that at home but not in court. Take it back. Withdrawn.
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting violent punishment for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual parenting advice about context-appropriate violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good character work - Homer's terrible parenting logic is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Well, McBain, you certainly picked a bad time to come out of retirement. I hope you have a COBRA plan.
jokes.punchline: COBRA as both snake/villain reference and health insurance
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay combining action movie tropes with mundane health insurance reality

jokes.text: You are suffering from a reptile dysfunction.
jokes.punchline: Reptile dysfunction instead of erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Basic pun - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Down in front. Damn it, why do kids have heads?
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical complaint about children's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - blaming fundamental human anatomy for his inconvenience

jokes.text: Oh, but I'm invested in the characters. You'll miss the turnoff to the fancy new mall. I make my own turnoffs.
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of 'making turnoffs'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with characteristic Homer stubbornness

jokes.text: And that, kids, is how you stick it to the Towne Centre Real Estate Investment Trust.
jokes.punchline: Overly specific corporate name deflates the rebellion
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate naming conventions undermining grass-roots rebellion

jokes.text: Someone thinks we're leaving. I'll wave them off. No, no, no, no, let's see how long we can keep 'em waiting. Everyone act like we're buckling up to go home.
jokes.setup: Family preparing to leave parking spot
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty cruelty to waiting driver
jokes.explanation: Relatable petty behavior elevated by Homer's gleeful orchestration

jokes.text: Marge, if you were married to Da Vinci, you wouldn't tell him not to Da Vinch.
jokes.punchline: Homer turning Da Vinci into a verb to defend his actions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - creating a verb from Da Vinci to justify petty parking lot behavior. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Damn it, are you leaving or not?! Leaving? Why on earth would you think that?
jokes.setup: Frustrated driver asking if they're leaving
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent confusion after deliberate trolling
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the parking spot setup - Homer's fake innocence works well

jokes.text: We totally wasted his time. And ours.
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical assessment of mutual time waste
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge reality check - functional but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: This place is so great. When the trolley hits you, it doesn't even hurt.
jokes.punchline: Getting hit by trolley as positive feature
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic about mall safety - decent Bart-style observation

jokes.text: You try it, Maggie.
jokes.setup: Bart encouraging Maggie after praising painless trolley hits
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting baby should get hit by trolley
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to trolley joke - fits Bart's character but not exceptional

jokes.text: Lenny, do you live here? Yeah, I just moved in. I have a Dippin' Dots for breakfast every morning; at lunch, I get a massage in front of strangers; and then I spend the afternoon browsing cell phone skins.
jokes.setup: Question about Lenny living at the mall
jokes.punchline: Lenny's sad routine of mall-based lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Good satire of consumer culture and mall life - Lenny's pathetic but specific routine

jokes.text: Ever get tired of those dancing waters? The day I moved in.
jokes.punchline: Lenny's immediate disillusionment
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - the speed of Lenny's disillusionment is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Give me one doll from every time period, and set me up for a tea party in a private room.
jokes.punchline: Adult man requesting elaborate doll tea party
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy - Lenny's loneliness manifesting in concerning ways

jokes.text: Hi, dear, I'm your Uncle Lenny. I'm going to buy you an all-new wardrobe, fix your teeth, send you to the best schools, and you're going to hate and resent me for it 'cause you're an American now.
jokes.setup: Lenny addressing Ellis Island Emily doll
jokes.punchline: Predicting the immigrant experience of prosperity leading to resentment
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated dark humor about the American Dream and generational conflict - surprisingly deep for a throwaway moment

jokes.text: D'oh! Do-over.
jokes.setup: Homer making mistake with gift cards
jokes.punchline: Calling for do-over like a child
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer childishness - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Sir, as a complexion scientist, I must advise you against using any more. This is the most powerful hydrator we sell. Less blathering, more slathering.
jokes.punchline: Homer's rhyming dismissal of medical advice
jokes.explanation: Solid rhyming pun with typical Homer disregard for consequences

jokes.text: Yo, Bart dude. Can I get a ride to the food court? Hop on.
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of Nelson riding Bart's skateboard or similar
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag - functional but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: I kind of want to create my own thing. Do you sell any just plain sets? No. We do all the imagining for you.
jokes.punchline: Store's elimination of imagination
jokes.explanation: Good satire of commercialized creativity and pre-packaged play

jokes.text: You do and you'd better build yourself a lawyer.
jokes.punchline: Legal threat using building metaphor
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with corporate legal threats - good economy

jokes.text: Trust me, I've left a lot out and cleaned up the swears.
jokes.punchline: Lisa acknowledging she's censoring her family's behavior
jokes.explanation: Lisa's self-aware commentary about her family's awfulness - good character voice

jokes.text: Lisa, how can we put this? You're the reason no one wears Silly Bandz anymore.
jokes.setup: Girls explaining why Lisa can't join them
jokes.punchline: Blaming Lisa for killing a trend
jokes.explanation: Effective mean girl humor with timely reference - cruel but specific

jokes.text: Wha...? They, they don't?
jokes.setup: Lisa learning she killed Silly Bandz
jokes.punchline: Lisa's confusion and embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to previous joke - Lisa's social cluelessness showing

jokes.text: What the...?
jokes.setup: Learning about Silly Bandz being uncool
jokes.punchline: Lisa's confusion and dismay
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat that serves the story but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I'll take it, provided you charge me for services that Google offers for free. I already have. Sweet.
jokes.punchline: Requesting to pay more for free services
jokes.explanation: Good tech industry satire about paying premiums for basic services

jokes.text: This computer is so great. I'm watching the latest Sofia Coppola movie at 20x speed to make it seem like a normal movie.
jokes.punchline: Speeding up notoriously slow-paced films
jokes.explanation: Clever film criticism disguised as tech praise - specific cultural reference executed well

jokes.text: I think it just froze. Oh, no, no, that bird just moved.
jokes.punchline: Realizing Sofia Coppola film is just extremely slow
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback extending the Sofia Coppola joke - good structure and economy

jokes.text: I have no friends.
jokes.punchline: Blunt statement of social isolation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for episode's main plot - economical character moment

jokes.text: I'd have more friends if I knew what people liked, but I won't know what people like unless I'm their friend. It's a conundrum. If you want friends, don't use words like 'conundrum.' Co-what-drum?
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical advice and mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Lisa's overthinking vs Homer's blunt social wisdom

jokes.text: Co-what-drum?
jokes.punchline: Homer mispronouncing the word he just criticized
jokes.explanation: Homer's hypocrisy revealed through mispronunciation - perfect economy and character consistency

jokes.text: Jimbo heard me say it. I'm dead.
jokes.punchline: Immediate social doom from vocabulary choice
jokes.explanation: Good immediate consequence to previous joke - escalates Lisa's social anxiety

jokes.text: It's easier to be friends with lots of people online than one person in person.
jokes.punchline: Insight about online vs offline relationships
jokes.explanation: Basic social media observation - functional plot setup but not particularly insightful

jokes.text: How's this for a conundrum? It's not really a conundrum. Sit at my feet and I will elaborate.
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's condescending correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy response - pedantic and superior as expected

jokes.text: Life is too dang short. With a attitude like that, I wouldn't be her friend.
jokes.punchline: Judge piling on to friendless child
jokes.explanation: Mild judicial unprofessionalism joke - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: As the CEO of SpringFace, I want you all to have fun as you write source code until you fall asleep at your consoles.
jokes.punchline: Corporate doublespeak about worker exploitation
jokes.explanation: Good tech industry satire about toxic work culture disguised as fun

jokes.text: Here's my favorite computer game: Angry Nerds.
jokes.punchline: Angry Birds parody with nerds
jokes.explanation: Basic parody name - predictable and lazy

jokes.text: The only way I would be your friend is if I could click a box under your picture saying 'Accept friendship request from.'
jokes.punchline: Preferring digital to real friendship
jokes.explanation: Decent social media insight from unexpected character - Nelson's antisocial nature working digitally

jokes.text: If we don't move, his odds of getting us both are 374 to one.
jokes.punchline: Mathematical calculation of bullying odds
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - Database's analytical approach to playground violence

jokes.text: Oh, I just got invited to make out with Shauna. See? That went out to 200 guys. And seven girls.
jokes.punchline: Mass invitation to everyone
jokes.explanation: Good social media behavior satire - mass messaging undermining apparent specialness

jokes.text: Santa's Little Helper's friends with Snowball Two? Now I've seen everything.
jokes.punchline: Pet friendship as mind-blowing development
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist take on pet social networking - Homer's easy amazement

jokes.text: Ha, all my friends have birthdays this year.
jokes.punchline: Stating obvious fact as remarkable
jokes.explanation: Simple Barney logic - stating universal truth as personal discovery

jokes.text: I'm less popular than the hornet's nest in the gym. You said you were getting rid of that nest. We trade the honey for chalk and yardsticks. Hornets make honey? Better than wasp honey, not as good as bee.
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity of hornet honey trading and quality rankings
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity - starts with simple complaint, builds to bizarre honey taxonomy

jokes.text: Hornets make honey? Better than wasp honey, not as good as bee.
jokes.punchline: Skinner's detailed knowledge of various insect honeys
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the insect honey absurdity - great character commitment to bizarre logic

jokes.text: Is this how you talk on dates? I wish my dates were this interesting.
jokes.punchline: Skinner's sad dating life confession
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Skinner's social awkwardness extending to romance

jokes.text: We've got to thank you, kiddo. We've gotten so much more action since we signed up and used this picture of ourselves. That's not you. You can see our reflection in the sunglasses.
jokes.setup: Twins thanking Lisa for dating success
jokes.punchline: Lisa spotting their catfishing attempt
jokes.explanation: Good catfishing joke with visual element - Patty and Selma's desperation in character

jokes.text: And I've created something that created something incredibly popular. And I created an alcoholic hippo. You never showed it to me. A stupid alcoholic hippo. I still want to see it. There is no hippo. Then why did you say it? 'Cause you're the hippo. Are you just saying that 'cause you don't want me to see the hippo? I don't have a hippo!
jokes.punchline: Escalating argument about non-existent alcoholic hippo
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - starts with Homer's delusion, spirals into marital confusion over fictional hippo

jokes.text: Man, this Web site makes talking drunk to my wife so much safer. I am sitting here... zero sheets to the wind... counting the moments to closing time when I can stumble home to you. Another round, Moe.
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by ordering another drink
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Moe's self-deception and alcoholism on display

jokes.text: Uh-oh, did I type that? Delete! Delete! Hm, typing 'delete' does not delete.
jokes.setup: Moe realizing he sent wrong message
jokes.punchline: Not understanding how computers work
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Moe's technical incompetence - simple but effective

jokes.text: It is gratifying to see all of you bowing in prayer, the light of God shining on your faces.
jokes.punchline: Mistaking phone screens for divine light
jokes.explanation: Good modern technology meets religion joke - Lovejoy's misinterpretation is believable

jokes.text: Uh-oh, Bernice Hibbert keeps 'liking' Bumblebee Man'sos. That's how it starts.
jokes.punchline: Recognizing signs of affair through likes
jokes.explanation: Decent social media behavior observation - religious leader as relationship expert

jokes.text: Why did I make this church a Wi-Fi hotspot? If you can't beat 'em, join 'em. Let's see, which Muppet am I? Beaker? Hm, I guess that's fair.
jokes.punchline: Taking Muppet quiz and accepting unflattering result
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor - Lovejoy's resignation to being boring like Beaker

jokes.text: Oh, I have a thousand friends, and I feel more alone than ever. Whee! I am trying to set a mood here. Whee.
jokes.punchline: Maggie's oblivious playground joy interrupting
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of existential angst with innocent play - timing works well

jokes.text: just like the 60-foot baby in my self-published novel, The 60 Ft. Baby. Order it online now, while you can still cheat the government on sales tax.
jokes.punchline: Shameless self-promotion plus tax evasion advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy behavior - shameless self-promotion with typical obsessive specificity

jokes.text: Check this out: I'm hiding a bomb in this pile of corpses, so when Kearney loots their ammo, he gets a face full of ass shrapnel. Damn, I got ass-shrapped!
jokes.punchline: Creating portmanteau for buttocks-based explosion damage
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor with mild wordplay - appropriate for characters but not particularly clever

jokes.text: That idiot cut me off. I'm gonna run his plates, find out who he is, then change his SpringFace profile picture to a shot of a monkey. Get that, Marge? A monkey!
jokes.punchline: Disproportionate social media revenge plus proud explanation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from road rage to petty digital revenge - Homer's pride in mild prank is perfect

jokes.text: Status update: Homer... is... watching... the...
jokes.punchline: Implied crash from distracted driving
jokes.explanation: Predictable distracted driving gag - functional physical comedy but not original

jokes.text: Somebody call 911! I don't know how to use the phone on my phone.
jokes.punchline: Can't operate basic phone functions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - police chief unable to use basic emergency functions

jokes.text: Hey, did you guys get my picture of the fire? Yeah, I did; nice grab. I didn't get it yet. Yeah, funny how that works.
jokes.punchline: Treating disaster as social media content
jokes.explanation: Dark social media commentary - prioritizing content over helping, but execution is ordinary

jokes.text: I know it's awful and insular and caused 35 deaths, but I had friends. Four digits of friends! I had a friend in common with Malcolm Gladwell. He friends everybody.
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing shallow connections over human cost plus Gladwell reference
jokes.explanation: Good social media addiction portrayal - Lisa's moral blindness for digital validation plus specific cultural reference

jokes.text: We don't need your crummy Web site. We can make anything into guns.
jokes.punchline: Preferring real-world violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about youth violence - character appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Want to play Marco Polo with us? We just realized you don't have to play in a pool. You really want me? No. We want your Dad.
jokes.punchline: Actually wanting Homer instead
jokes.explanation: Standard rejection joke with mild twist - functional but predictable cruelty

jokes.text: We want your Dad. Marco. Come on, Lisa. Polo! Fish out of water.
jokes.setup: Lisa thinking kids want to include her
jokes.punchline: They only want Homer for their game
jokes.explanation: Lisa's continued social rejection - predictable but maintains character arc

jokes.text: Hey, Winklevosses. You're gonna lose yet again. You should see the expression on your faces. Oh, wait, you can. They're fat, they smoke, they started training a week ago. Why can't we pull away?
jokes.punchline: Despite advantages, still losing to inferior competition
jokes.explanation: Topical Facebook reference but execution is workmanlike - competent but not inspired

jokes.text: Because we can't stop concentrating on that $65 million Facebook settlement, which somehow wasn't enough for us. Even though we were rich in the first place.
jokes.punchline: Greed despite already being wealthy
jokes.explanation: Direct social commentary on wealth inequality and greed - heavy-handed but topical

jokes.text: Once there was a young rascal named Bart. This lad was trouble from the very start.
jokes.punchline: Traditional rhyming introduction
jokes.explanation: Standard storybook opening - functional framing device but not comedically interesting

jokes.text: When it came to mischief, he had a black thumb.
jokes.punchline: Black thumb instead of green thumb
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay substitution - economical but predictable

jokes.text: It didn't hurt that his daddy was dumb.
jokes.punchline: Homer's stupidity as enabler
jokes.explanation: Direct character observation - true but unoriginal

jokes.text: Only to wait for Milhouse's bladder.
jokes.punchline: Bathroom break interrupting criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Typical Milhouse weakness interrupting adventure - character appropriate but predictable

jokes.text: They tried to escape with struggles and squirms. But all they got was a diet of worms.
jokes.punchline: Being fed worms as punishment
jokes.explanation: Dark ending to children's tale - conventional moral fable structure

jokes.text: There they remained to this very day. So now you know, pranks do not pay.
jokes.punchline: Heavy-handed anti-prank moral
jokes.explanation: Standard moral fable ending - predictable and heavy-handed

tmdb_overview: Lisa is called to trial after her online social networking site creates dysfunction in the community; Patty and Selma compete in a rowing event.
